mnemonics: CNN: Cats Need Napkins (for Convolution, Non-linearity, Pooling layers)
RNN: Remembering New Notes (for processing sequential data like text or time series)
FFNN: Fast Food Needs Napkins (Input Layer, Hidden Layers, Output Layer like a simple burger)
keyPoints: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) excel at processing grid-like data (e.g., images) using spatial hierarchies.
Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are designed for sequential data, using their internal state (memory) to process variable-length sequences.
Fully Connected Feedforward Networks (FFNNs) form the fundamental building block, transforming input through hidden layers to an output.
commonMistakes: Using a standard FFNN for image processing, which ignores spatial structure and is computationally inefficient.
Applying a CNN to non-grid structured data (like text without embedding), for which it is not well-suited.
Forgetting to use techniques like gradient clipping or LSTMs/GRUs to combat the vanishing/exploding gradient problem in RNNs.
